subject: First Aid & Safety
title: How Blood Clots Form
description: One-minute skill: How Blood Clots Form.

hook.text: You cut your finger. Within 3 minutes, a clot has sealed the wound. A 12-step chemical cascade made that happen.

buildup.text: Platelets arrive first, sticking to exposed collagen and forming a temporary plug.

discovery.text: The coagulation cascade activates 12 clotting factors in a precise sequence to build a fibrin mesh.

twist.text: Hemophilia patients are missing just one factor in the cascade—and it's enough to cause life-threatening bleeding.

climax.text: This same system can form dangerous clots inside blood vessels—causing strokes and heart attacks.

punchline.text: 12 factors. One precise sequence. Miss one and you could bleed out.

quiz.question: What do platelets do first at a wound site?
options: Stick to exposed collagen and form a temporary plug
